title: boronia pilosa subsp. torquata
intro: Boronia pilosa subsp. torquata is a subspecies of flowering plant in the citrus family, Rutaceae, native to the southwestern region of Western Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in Western Australian Aboriginal medicine, where its leaves, flowers, and roots are used to treat various ailments due to its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Boronia pilosa subsp. torquata include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate skin conditions and wounds, as well as its potential antiseptic properties to prevent infection. Boronia pilosa subsp. torquata can be prepared as an herbal tea, infused oil, or tincture, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
